title: How Creators in Ghana Sell Digital Products: Which Option Works Regardless of Where You Are
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Creators in Nigeria, Pakistan, Ghana, Bangladesh, and dozens of other countries cannot use the platforms everyone else takes for granted. This changes that.

content.text: As a creator in Ghana, you've poured your heart and soul into crafting a digital product, only to find that traditional payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe won't let you collect payments from international customers. It's frustrating to have a great product and a willing audience, but no way to get paid. You're not alone in this struggle, and it's time to explore alternative solutions that can help you sell your digital products globally without geographic restrictions.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When evaluating payment solutions for your digital products, you're comparing more than just fees and features - you're comparing the ability to reach a global audience versus being limited to a local market. Traditional platforms like Gumroad and Payhip may seem convenient, but they often come with restrictions that can hinder your growth as a creator. On the other hand, crypto-native infrastructure offers a more flexible and inclusive alternative.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Crypto-native infrastructure has no country-level exclusions because it has no country-level compliance layer. A creator in Lagos and a creator in Berlin access the same NOWPayments API, configure the same payment coins, and receive funds to the same type of wallet. The transaction is verified by the blockchain, not by a compliance team reviewing account applications by geography. The only thing that matters is whether the payment confirms on chain. If it does, the product delivers. The creator in the restricted country has exactly the same infrastructure capabilities as the creator in any supported country. This is not a workaround. It is infrastructure designed without the geographic assumption that creates the problem in the first place.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, offers a more decentralized and inclusive approach to payment processing. With platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can sell your digital products and receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors or banks. This approach can help you reach a global audience and collect payments from customers all over the world, regardless of where you are located.
The ability to collect payments from international customers is the lifeblood of any digital product business, and crypto-native infrastructure can provide a reliable and secure way to do so.
The Real Cost Difference

While traditional payment platforms may seem cheaper at first glance, the real cost difference lies in the restrictions and limitations they impose on creators. With crypto-native infrastructure, you may pay a slightly higher fee, but you gain the ability to reach a global audience and collect payments from customers all over the world. This can lead to significant revenue gains and opportunities that would be impossible with traditional platforms.
Which One to Use and Why

If you're a creator in Ghana or another restricted country, using crypto-native infrastructure can be a game-changer for your business. With platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can sell your digital products and receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors or banks. This approach can help you reach a global audience and collect payments from customers all over the world, regardless of where you are located.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

As a creator in a restricted country, you want to know how to sell digital products globally without geographic restrictions. The answer lies in using crypto-native infrastructure, which can help you reach a global audience and collect payments from customers all over the world. Whether you're in Ghana, Pakistan, or another country, you can use platforms like Unchained Commerce to sell your digital products and receive payments in a variety of cryptocurrencies, without the need for traditional payment processors or banks.
Using crypto-native infrastructure can help you avoid the restrictions and limitations imposed by traditional payment platforms, and reach a global audience with your digital products.
Related Topics Worth Reading

Selling ebooks from Africa using crypto, receiving international payments for digital products from Pakistan, and selling online from a country that Stripe does not support are all possible with crypto-native infrastructure. By using platforms like Unchained Commerce, you can reach a global audience and collect payments from customers all over the world, regardless of where you are located.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your digital product store, make sure you have a solid foundation in place. This includes setting up a Core Shop Engine to handle automated delivery and payment verification, integrating Web3 Wallet Connect to enable multi-chain QR codes and live price feeds, and using an Admin Dashboard to track transaction logs and order management. By taking these steps, you can create a seamless and secure payment experience for your customers and set yourself up for success in the global market.

Success as a digital product creator means being able to sell your products and receive payments from customers all over the world, without being held back by geographic restrictions or traditional payment platforms.
